message:
feat: scheduler min-heap ordering, extension shutdown robustness, JS polyfills, JPEG parser hardening, and package list fast path

Scheduler (src/scheduler.rs):
- Replace VecDeque macrotask queue with BinaryHeap<Reverse<Macrotask>> to
  enforce strict monotone sequence ordering (invariant I5) even when timers
  fire concurrently with queued events — previously, move_due_timers reused
  timer-original seq values which could violate total ordering when interleaved
  with newer macrotasks
- Due timers now receive fresh macrotask seq via next_seq() so execution order
  is always globally monotone while preserving (deadline, timer-seq) firing
  priority within the timer heap itself
- Add scheduler_due_timers_do_not_preempt_queued_macrotasks test proving that
  an event enqueued before a timer fires always executes first
- Include scheduler_repro.rs reproduction test from concurrent agent

Extension runtime (src/extensions.rs):
- Handle the case where the JS runtime exit signal channel is closed (sender
  dropped) before the shutdown ack arrives — this indicates the runtime is
  already gone, so treat it as successful cleanup rather than a timeout
- Log the dropped-signal case at warn level for postmortem visibility
- Add runtime_shutdown_treats_closed_exit_signal_as_success test

JS runtime polyfills (src/extensions_js.rs):
- Add require.resolve() and require.resolve.paths() stubs to the PiJS
  createRequire polyfill — extensions like @sourcegraph/scip-python call
  require.resolve() to locate binary entry points and would throw without this
- Expand @aliou/pi-utils-settings ConfigLoader to match real API (constructor
  takes name+defaultConfig+options, async load(), getConfig() accessor)
- Expand @marckrenn/pi-sub-shared to include 5 providers (adding google, aws,
  azure), proper PROVIDER_METADATA and PROVIDER_DISPLAY_NAMES factories
- Add @sourcegraph/scip-python and /index.js module stubs

Tools (src/tools.rs):
- Fix BashTool: is_error was always false even when result.cancelled or
  exit_code != 0 — now correctly propagates the error flag to ToolOutput
- Flatten nested if-let chains in GrepTool and FindTool gitignore discovery
  using let-chains (modern Rust style)
- LsTool: handle broken symlinks gracefully via is_ok_and() instead of
  skipping entries with metadata errors

Session (src/session.rs):
- Simplify session file loading with map_while(Result::ok) instead of nested
  for+if-let for JSONL line iteration
- Minor formatting cleanup in async file open

Terminal images (src/terminal_images.rs):
- Rewrite JPEG dimension parser to be fully robust: handles 0xFF fill bytes
  between markers, recognizes all SOF marker variants (0xC0-0xC3, 0xC5-0xC7,
  0xC9-0xCB, 0xCD-0xCF), properly skips standalone markers (RST, TEM),
  terminates on SOS/EOI before SOF, validates segment lengths
- Extract is_jpeg_sof_marker() const fn for clarity
- Add tests for fill-byte handling and extended SOF marker support

Package manager & main (src/package_manager.rs, src/main.rs):
- Add list_packages_blocking() and installed_path_blocking() sync wrappers
  for startup fast paths that don't require the async runtime
- Add handle_package_list_blocking() and print_package_entry_blocking() to
  support the sync path
- Move `pi list` to the same early-exit fast path as `pi config`, bypassing
  async runtime initialization for faster CLI response

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
